# sourcevision

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 141
- **Lines of code**: 45,901

- **Zones**: 9 (9 with descriptions)
- **Git**: main @ 63d150a
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-08T06:07:28.248Z

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- src/analyzers/ directory spans files from five zone identifiers (analyzers, analyzers-2, analyzers-3, cli, unit). This directory-to-zone mismatch is the root cause of 8 findings being flagged with inflated severity. Introducing subdirectories within src/analyzers/ (e.g. src/analyzers/zones/, src/analyzers/imports/, src/analyzers/branch/) would allow future Louvain passes to produce zone boundaries that match the intended domain structure, eliminating the partition artifact problem at its source. [warning]
- God function: registerMcpTools in src/cli/mcp.ts calls 36 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions [warning]

### Patterns

- Zone finding 1 (coupling warning 0.52, 15 imports to analyzers) is a Louvain partitioning artifact corroborated by global finding 10. The 15 crossings are intra-domain edges within one coherent analyzers domain that the algorithm split across zone boundaries. No refactoring of coupling is warranted; the actionable fix is structural (subdirectory organization of src/analyzers/). [info] (analyzers-2)
- Zone finding 0 (coupling warning to analyzers-2) is confirmed as a false positive by two independent sources: zone finding 1 (LLM) and global finding 10 (heuristic cross-check). E2e tests importing from the analyzers domain is correct by design. Severity should be treated as info. [info] (e2e)
- Zone finding 0 (warning: coupling 0.57 to 'analyzers-2') is contradicted by global finding 10. E2e tests coupling to what is semantically one analyzers domain is expected; the warning severity is not justified. The coupling flag measures intra-domain edges that Louvain partitioned into separate zone identifiers, not genuine cross-concern dependencies. [info] (e2e)
- Eight findings were downgraded from warning to info because their severity was derived solely from Louvain zone partition artifacts rather than genuine architectural problems. The metrics (coupling 0.52–0.69, cohesion 0.31, 'critical risk' score) measured intra-domain edge crossings within src/analyzers/ that the community detection algorithm assigned to separate zone identifiers. None of these metrics reflect cross-concern dependencies in the architectural sense. [info]
- Execution sequence for all confirmed actionable findings, in dependency order: (1) grep and remove detectBranchLifecycle and resolveWorkedEpicTitlesForRange — independent, no downstream risk; (2) audit 6 production-file imports from src/analyzers/ that cross into the unit/test layer — independent of step 1; (3) if step 2 finds shared declarations in test-only files, extract them to src/types/ or src/shared/; (4) decompose registerMcpTools into three sequential named sub-registrar functions within src/cli/mcp.ts — independent of steps 1–3, can proceed in parallel; (5) apply subdirectory grouping within src/analyzers/ after step 3 completes to resolve Louvain partition artifacts. Steps 1, 2, and 4 have no dependencies and may proceed simultaneously. [info]
- Global finding 0 (bidirectional coupling warning) directly contradicts global findings 10 and 13 (LLM analysis). Finding 13 explicitly resolves the contradiction: the 27-crossing unit→analyzers direction is expected test coupling that must not be flagged as warning; only the 7-crossing analyzers→unit direction is a genuine concern, already captured with full specificity by global finding 5. Global finding 0 is superseded and its warning severity is incorrect. [info]
- Three findings (cli finding 1, cli finding 2, global finding 14) independently identify the same root cause — registerMcpTools fan-out of 36 — through different analysis methods (LLM structural review, LLM synthesis, automated call-graph counting). This triple corroboration from independent sources elevates confidence beyond any other finding in the package. It is the only finding that satisfies the multi-source corroboration standard required for a warning recommendation. [warning]
- Zone metrics (cohesion, coupling, risk score) are unreliable for zones whose files have zero import edges to each other — this includes the .claude and root zones. Applying the 0.50 at-risk threshold to these zones produces systematic false positives because the score reflects an undefined-input fallback rather than a measured architectural property. These zones should be filtered from risk-score ranking outputs. [info]

### Observations

- Isolated files — no import edges between 2 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0) [info] (.claude)
- 16 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports [warning] (analyzers)
- Generic zone name "Analyzers 2" — enrichment did not assign a meaningful name reflecting this zone's domain purpose [warning] (analyzers-2)
- High coupling (0.52) — 15 imports target "analyzers" [warning] (analyzers-2)
- Generic zone name "Analyzers 3" — enrichment did not assign a meaningful name reflecting this zone's domain purpose [warning] (analyzers-3)
- High coupling (0.69) — 10 imports target "analyzers" [warning] (analyzers-3)
- Low cohesion (0.31) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone [warning] (analyzers-3)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (e2e)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (fixtures)
- Bidirectional coupling: "analyzers" ↔ "unit" (7+27 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- ... and 5 more

### Suggestions

- Zone "analyzers" has files across 9 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (analyzers)
- Zone "cli" has files across 7 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (cli)
- Decompose registerMcpTools in src/cli/mcp.ts into three named sub-registrar functions called in sequence: one for analysis tools (sv_inventory, sv_imports, sv_zones, sv_components, sv_context), one for status/reporting tools (rex_status, rex_next, rex_validate), and one for workflow tools (rex_add, rex_update, rex_analyze, rex_recommend). All three remain in mcp.ts; registerMcpTools becomes a three-call orchestrator. This is the only file-level change required. [info] (cli)
- Remove detectBranchLifecycle from src/analyzers/branch-work-filter.ts and resolveWorkedEpicTitlesForRange from src/cli/commands/prd-epic-resolver.ts — both exports are confirmed dead (no callers found anywhere in the package, only declaration sites). Removal is low-risk and independent of all other findings. [warning] (cli)
- registerMcpTools in src/cli/mcp.ts (36 outgoing calls) should be decomposed into focused sub-registrar functions grouped by capability domain (e.g. registration of analysis tools, status tools, and workflow tools as separate named functions called in sequence). This is an in-file decomposition of the single oversized function — not a file split — which reduces call-graph fan-out and isolates the impact of future tool additions without changing the module's public API. [info] (cli)
- registerMcpTools in src/cli/mcp.ts is the highest-priority actionable finding in this package, corroborated independently by automated fan-out analysis (36 outgoing calls, global finding 11) and LLM analysis (cli finding 1). Decompose into three sequential named sub-registrar functions within the same file — one per capability domain (analysis tools, status/reporting tools, workflow tools) — called in sequence from registerMcpTools. This is an in-file decomposition only; no file split is required or warranted at this threshold. [warning] (cli)
- Zone "fixtures" has files across 5 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (fixtures)
- src/analyzers/ contains files from 5 zones (analyzers, analyzers-2, analyzers-3, cli, unit) — consider grouping into subdirectories to clarify architectural boundaries [info]
- Recommended execution sequence for remaining actionable findings, accounting for dependencies: (1) grep for detectBranchLifecycle and resolveWorkedEpicTitlesForRange project-wide and remove if unused — independent, under five minutes each; (2) audit the 6 specific production-file imports from src/analyzers/ that cross into the unit/test layer — independent of step 1; (3) if step 2 finds shared declarations in test-only files, extract them to src/types/ or src/shared/ before proceeding; (4) decompose registerMcpTools in src/cli/mcp.ts into three sequential sub-registrar functions — independent of steps 1–3; (5) apply subdirectory grouping within src/analyzers/ — depends on step 3 being complete. Steps 1, 2, and 4 can proceed in parallel. [info]
- Hub function: info in src/cli/output.ts is called from 9 files (high fan-in expected for this module type) — changes here have wide impact, consider if responsibilities can be narrowed [info]
- ... and 6 more

| `tests/unit/cli/git-credential-helper.test.ts` | test |  | cli |
| `tests/unit/cli/help.test.ts` | test |  | cli |
| `tests/unit/cli/mcp.test.ts` | test |  | unit |
| `tests/unit/cli/output.test.ts` | test |  | cli |
| `tests/unit/cli/pr-markdown.test.ts` | test |  | cli |
| `tests/unit/cli/prd-epic-resolver.test.ts` | test |  | cli |
| `tests/unit/cli/workspace.test.ts` | test |  | unit |
| `tests/unit/export/pdf-report.test.ts` | test |  | unit |
| `tests/unit/generators/pr-markdown-template.test.ts` | test |  | unit |
| `tests/unit/schema/data-files.test.ts` | test |  | unit |
| `tests/unit/schema/validate.test.ts` | test |  | e2e |
| `tests/unit/util/merge.test.ts` | test |  | analyzers |
| `tests/unit/util/sort.test.ts` | test |  | analyzers |
| `tsconfig.json` | config |  | root |
| `tsconfig.tsbuildinfo` | config |  | root |
| `vitest.config.ts` | config |  | root |

## Tooling

This project uses n-dx for codebase analysis (Sourcevision) and requirement
tracking (Rex). MCP tools are available for interactive access to analysis
data and PRD management. Skills available: /plan, /status, /capture,
/zone, /work, /configure.
